name: INGOP
subject: Carrying Our Momentum
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
Dear Hoosier Republicans, No images? [Click here](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-e-akdbil-hhlljjcji-jd/) _**Carrying Our Momentum**_ Dear Hoosier Republicans,Congratulations to all of our Republican primary winners across Indiana following last week's elections, and thank you to all those Hoosier Republicans who showed up to make your voice heard. We have once again demonstrated that our party is energized, united around conservative values, and ready to deliver results for families across our state.Now it is time to come together as Republicans and focus on the mission ahead: protecting our majorities, supporting President Trump and Governor Braun's conservative leadership, and continuing to move Indiana forward.

Indiana remains one of the strongest Republican states in the nation because Hoosier voters believe in lower taxes, safer communities, and economic opportunity. Now it's up to us to carry that momentum into November.As always, thank you for being a part of our team, and please feel free to reach out if there's any way I can be of assistance.Yours in service, Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman _**Governor Braun Protects Hoosier Foster Care Youth, Indiana Families**_ Governor Mike Braun signed an executive order to ensure federal survivor benefits belonging to children in foster care are protected and preserved for their future use, rather than used to offset state costs. The order also expands financial protections, transparency, and financial literacy resources for foster youth as they prepare for adulthood. Governor Braun also joined federal officials in launching Indiana's participation in the "A Home for Every Child" initiative aimed at improving safety, permanency, and well-being outcomes for children in foster care."It is very simple, survivor benefits belong to the child as intended, not the government. This executive order improves DCS services for Hoosier families and makes sure those dollars are protected and used to support young Hoosiers as they prepare for adulthood."\- Governor Braun via a [press release](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-r/) _**Governor Braun Celebrates Major Win as Fortune 500 Corteva Picks Indiana for Crop Protection HQ**_ Governor Mike Braun announced that Corteva's selection of Indianapolis for the global headquarters of its new crop protection and biological technologies company, beating out competing states for the investment. The decision not only strengthens Indiana's position as a national leader in agbioscience, but will also create and support high-quality jobs and long-term economic growth. State and local leaders celebrated the move as a reflection of Indiana's workforce, business climate, and growing life sciences and agriculture sectors."Indiana is a global leader in agbioscience - and today's announcement is just more proof. Corteva's decision to base its global headquarters here reinforces our national reputation as an innovation corridor, nurturing a world-leading bioscience industry that supports hundreds of thousands of Hoosier jobs and generates billions of dollars for the state's economy. Indiana continues its winning streak."\- Governor Braun via a [press release](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-y/) _**Governor Braun Signs Legislation Supporting Long-Term Care Services for Hoosier Seniors**_ Governor Mike Braun ceremonially signed HEA 1277, legislation updating Indiana's long-term care system to better support Hoosier seniors while ensuring responsible use of Medicaid resources. The law expands support for assisted living and home-based care, improves transparency for patients and families, and aims to maintain access to quality care for elderly Hoosiers."Hoosiers deserve a long-term care system that is compassionate, sustainable, and focused on the needs of seniors. This law helps ensure Medicaid resources are used responsibly while supporting continued access to care for elderly Hoosiers."\- Governor Braun via a [press release](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-j/) [](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-t/) [Subscribe to The Braun Briefing!](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-i/) If you want to stay up to date with everything happening in the Governor's office, don't miss out, subscribe today! _**Chairwoman's County Visits: 36**_ **Vanderburgh County, Evansville** **2026 State Convention** [Click Here for More Information](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-d/) _**Indiana GOP is Hiring Interns for the Summer & Fall**_ [Click Here to Apply](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-h/) **📱 _News You Can Use_ ** **Governor Mike Braun**** **[signed](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-k/) Senate Enrolled Act 78, a new 'bell-to-bell' cell phone restriction strengthening Indiana's school cell phone policies to help students focus, support teachers, and improve the classroom environment.**Lieutenant Governor Micah Beckwith** [kicked](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-u/) off the Hoosier Leadership Series (HLS) 2026 class yesterday as one of the keynote speakers, saying, "I had the opportunity to speak on American Exceptionalism... what it means, why it matters, and why America is absolutely worth saving. Our nation isn't perfect, but it is uniquely rooted in the truth that our rights come from God, not government. That foundation is worth defending, preserving, and passing on to the next generation."**Secretary Diego Morales**[visited](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-o/) polling stations throughout the state on election day, saying,** "**THANK YOU to all poll workers, election administrators, and voters who made yesterday's Primary Election a success! Your dedication, hard work, and service to the Hoosier state is unmatched."**Attorney General Todd Rokita**[a](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-b/)[lleging](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-n/) that two companies have failed to employ sufficient protections against online predators, Attorney General Todd Rokita has filed a lawsuit against Roblox Corp., maker of a massive online gaming platform, and Discord Inc., which operates a messaging app. "These companies, which cater to kids and young individuals, know full well that numerous predatory sex criminals have used these platforms to contact and lure their victims," Attorney General Rokita said. "And yet they continue promoting themselves as safe for children. That is more than reckless. It's a clear and ongoing violation of Indiana's Deceptive Consumer Sales Act, and we are working to hold them accountable to protect Hoosier families."**Treasurer Daniel Elliott**[celebrated](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-p/) the National Day of Prayer, saying, "I'm thankful for God's blessings on our state and nation, and I'm reminded that prayer keeps us grounded through every season of life. I'd encourage every Hoosier to take a moment today to pray for our families, our communities, and our country."**Comptroller Elise Nieshalla**[alerted](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-x/) Hoosiers to the alarming news that US debt now outpaces GDP, saying, "This is a clear warning sign for our nation's future. It's time for Washington to follow the Hoosier model and turn our country's finances around."**Senator Todd Young**[co-sponsored](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-m/)** ** a resolution affirming concern over the growing threat China poses to America's national security and economic prosperity at a pivotal moment for U.S.-Chinese relations. The resolution calls for a continued and steady focus on strengthening deterrence in the Indo-Pacific, protecting American workers and businesses from unfair economic practices, maintaining leadership in artificial intelligence and other emerging technologies, supporting allies and partners, preserving peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait, and advancing human rights and democratic values. **Senator Jim Banks**[c](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-c/)[o-sponsored](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-q/) the _AI OVERWATCH Act_. This bill ensures that the chips powering the next generation of AI fuel American innovation and American national security, not the military and surveillance apparatus of the Chinese Communist Party. "We are in an AI arms race with the Chinese Communist Party that's powered by advanced chips and the winner will determine the future of global military superiority," said Senator Jim Banks. "I'm leading the AI OVERWATCH Act to bolster President Trump's national security strategy and ensure continued American AI dominance."**Congressman Rudy Yakym**[launched](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-a/) the bipartisan U.S.-Mexico Caucus. They will lead as co-chairs of the Caucus in the U.S. House of Representatives, which will focus on strengthening the relationship between the U.S. and Mexico and facilitating a dialogue on trade, security, and culture.**Congressman Marlin Stutzman **[celebrated](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-f/) the House Financial Services Committee's unanimous passage of the _FUTURES Act_ , legislation he co-led, which will enhance regulatory agencies' ability to conduct thorough supervision and ensure the safety and soundness of the financial system.**Congressman Jim Baird** voted in favor of H.R. 1346, the _Nationwide Consumer and Fuel Retailer Choice Act_ , saying, "For states like Indiana and districts like mine, this legislation is not just about fuel policy. Year-round E-15 nationwide will support the countless corn farmers in my district, strengthen our rural economies, and lower energy costs for hardworking families. This legislation will provide a strong, reliable market for corn farmers in Indiana, giving them more confidence to plan for the future."**Congresswoman Victoria Spartz**[celebrated](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-z/) inclusion of language she authored in the reauthorization of _Sec. 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA)_ , saying, "Protecting constitutional rights of Americans, including Fourth Amendment rights, is a primary responsibility of Congress. We know that in previous years, agencies were improperly collecting information on Americans using 'about' targeting, but no true audits were ever performed of Section 702 targeting. This new audit will, for the first time, help Congress better oversee how agencies target and collect."**Congressman Jefferson Shreve **[co-sponsored](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-v/) the _VERIFY Act,_ which would modernize the Department of Homeland Security's Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements system, known as SAVE, saying, "This is about accountability. When agencies issue commercial driver's licenses, they need accurate information without delay. Americans have died because of these failures. The SAVE system is outdated. The VERIFY Act brings it into the modern era and strengthens public safety."**Congressman Mark Messmer**[joined](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-e/) Chairman of the House Education and Workforce Committee, Rep. Tim Walberg, for a field hearing in Fort Branch, Indiana, titled "_Protecting Workers and Powering America: The Future of Mining._ " The hearing, held at the Vincennes University Gibson County Center for Advanced Manufacturing and Logistics, examined workforce development, mine safety, domestic energy production, and the future of the mining industry in Indiana and across the United States.**Congresswoman Erin Houchin**[co-sponsored](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-s/) the _Campus Lifeline Act of 2026_ , bipartisan legislation aiming to expand awareness and access to the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline for students and young adults, saying, "During Youth Mental Health Awareness Week, I'm proud to introduce the Campus Lifeline Act to expand access to the 988 Lifeline and strengthen support on campuses. This bill promotes early intervention, student engagement, and life-saving mental health resources. It's our goal to make sure every young person knows help is available." **_Upcoming Events_****May 30: [Lake County Lincoln Day Dinner](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-g/)****June 19-20: _[Indiana Republican State Convention 2026](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-w/)_****August 7: [Hancock County Elephant Open & Picnic](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-yd/)****August 15: [Dubois County Lincoln Day Dinner](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-yh/)****September 10: [Gibson County Lincoln Day Dinner](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-yk/)** [Click Here to Learn more about Governor Braun's Administrative Orders, Policy Proposals, and Leadership Appointments](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-yu/) [](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-jl/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-jr/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-jy/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-jj/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-jt/)[](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akdbil-hhlljjcji-ji/) ** PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE. ****NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. ****WWW.INDIANA.GOP.** | Indiana Republican State Committee 101 W. OHIO ST., SUITE 2200 INDIANAPOLIS, IN 46204 This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com [Preferences](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.updatemyprofile.com/y-akdbil-293A8D27-hhlljjcji-tl) | [Unsubscribe](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-u-akdbil-hhlljjcji-tr/)
---

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | A few people are collecting government checks that have nothing to do with taxes or Social Security. | No applications. ## CIA Director Flies to Cuba for Rare Talks
CIA Director John Ratcliffe [traveled to Havana](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGuqzAMQL8GtlQhAVKGDG9h61vfiExiGlchqRKXqn__RO8n3MWDLR2d43-35QYUKd3_wo6Wt7wkfNeIzFgWR-BeKxxUiesrJcLKr-qIFyXVKOQgunHxQPGz9K23G6JC3aLtjLlOapRmaIPtRzNJ14O-rs5jZ9D4FVfZTVqt2mnZkj1pcujGTkoz9Bd0nXf9cFWTVk6vpukleuJcLo9MiQNu8ZMPLBeX9zbawPysjf7TqLlRMwcMFON5a9Qc8o5nUKNmeH6HoMRYEjDlBPFndUYmcT-ZacfEosKnCkcgPBV0nIt45JBEAXaRtg3FjizexEHkbSNHEKugJAIckKBRc1usf74pRqrjqX_fgb5KLf_qx4dV_wMAAP__Z3mUZg) on Thursday for a rare high-level meeting with Cuban officials, delivering President Trump's message that the U.S. will engage "only if Cuba makes fundamental changes." Ratcliffe met with Raul Castro's grandson "Raulito," the Interior Minister, and the head of Cuban intelligence as the island faces its worst energy crisis in years. The country has reportedly run out of diesel and fuel oil, with [blackouts stretching 20-22 hours a day](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO4yAURb8Gd1jwwJAUFNu4y7ZbWhie47eDITI4Vv5-5PmFqa907r3n32N6eEqUn3_9hq4tZcp41oSt4T4F8uGY_ZsqtXrkTFjbUQO1CQQYLgYuzRQ9pc-ku-gWREDVoZPW3u5ghB261QVpolb6BoOVGCEuxghltfWoVIj23pG7aGKQRgphB91jkDHo4QZ3BUHNlmmBkVrZ-_-FcltxSZ_yxr0PZeuSW1t7Vab-MBgZjOd59iHP4QoZjBeawSgGBqPUDMbrEC-UeCSsmPhyYOJH5dXn0KjkyudUwpeP2K9tS93u4uuklKiaa8hz85R-ituvbL0dfAcAAP__8kV9eA) in some areas. Separately, the U.S. is [moving to indict former Cuban leader Raul Castro](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OrDAQhE8DmZHdYH4CBy8hm5duiEzTzPTK2CPcgOb2K_YKm5VUUlV99fWYHp4Dx-d_v5GTNU2RrhxIhPYJ2eMx-5MzSz5iZMpyZGSZQEOrtFWmnRbP4TM15eJWIqC6JGe6rh-g1Z0tX860QE1tPParxdrb3gCuA81o-76eB12yu9O0Na3RurNNRWgWbGwPQw1Yz13RaFpY0l59J47yojV80kl7hWkrg3uJvHNR_ytgLGC8rqvCOd8Ut1_AeMsCxiOrLZ0cn0qS4rgwirrpstr9ERT6LHtSaV0Z2Yessv8UMJa7W94Xh8C5vYc8N8_ht1j-9Nbp4CcAAP__7KyAZQ), 94, over Cuba's 1996 shootdown of two humanitarian aircraft operated by Brothers to the Rescue. The potential indictment still requires grand jury approval. The U.S. has also offered Cuba $100 million in humanitarian aid, conditioned on meaningful reforms. Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel said he'd prefer the U.S. simply lift the fuel blockade.
## Trump Touts Trade Deals from China Visit
President Trump wrapped up a two-day [state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u3CAUhJ8Gd6z4Nd6CIo27mzalheGsfSIMFhzvzb595LxC6pFm5vt-fS1fATOW7Wc4wNOrLgW-ewYiaEvEEK81vLEj9asUhE5Xj0iLEmrkwnI5Lilg_ixmSP4FoEAP4KVz01ONwtlh909r9eSikcFEs0b5dFpLB06Yl3YG1gH93SasHKUQzpoHRJmisZN6ahX16pgRkJBqe_yuWGiHV_7UN7RHrMeQ_U50dqZ_MDUzNdMOO-Z8Z0zNez3gBmJqDunAgp1aIKyFqdlOkxDCcmrXcfK4Ywn8D3JqIQFPEHLnawUsG9-AB2i1nyECU_PQfDq_MWfs431uOwL-Gxzovwy-vfobAAD___dvhdk) in Beijing Friday, calling it an "incredible visit" and saying the two sides made "fantastic trade deals, good for both countries." Details on the deals were sparse, but White House officials said China had agreed to purchase 200 Boeing jets, with a promise of up to 750 if things go well, along with 400-450 GE Aerospace engines and billions in U.S. soybeans. Beyond trade, Trump said Xi told him China [won't supply military equipment to Iran](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ_GdEb2YkxSuPgbuvztlcgxm7AnY5C9xJe3P5FXuG6kkebTN1-36eYpUnr-9ys6fmxTwloiMmOeAvlw3P2LCnE5UiIsfJRAPIECK1UvtZ1mT_E9mWZ2D0TArkGnh-FyBauGvllcgA6tVVZrNLPqdTDedh5UuFhjLQ4NuXNN9dpqpYbetBj0HEx_gWsHobsPwiicibfcfm-UeMFHfG8vzG3Y1ia6hXkvovsnYBQw1lrbcC-nxdkLGM8oYOR8rLus2e9FHrv8SMlKvMgfkpRkWCh5AWOT3bxXipGKPdHP1VP8oPhP_7wc_AYAAP__U316aQ), and that both leaders want the Strait of Hormuz reopened. Xi is expected to visit the White House in late September.
## Ranking: Sunniest Cities in the US
Yuma, Arizona [tops a new ranking of the sunniest U.S. cities](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFurDAQheGngQ6EB4O9hYvb0O1tUyJjD8tExiBmAO3bR5tXSH90Pun_eo5PT4ny679f0cm8jRlvTiiCxxjIh3PyFzEJnzkTspwcSEZooK-arlL9GD2l96jL6GZEwLZEp4yxD-gb05WLayN4C2ZCq60B5SNanGerjZ5UNGoqyX3emk71qmlMp2sMKgbdWXi0ENrJFLrBSLId9fdGWRac03u78KjDtpbJLSI7F-2_AoYChvu-64v49Cn4ncQnYvkMCxjCXsBwchVICLlaN5aKz8wLZSxgKA8X95tSIu4_5mv1lH4N-VOYy8FPAAAA___6JnkX), soaking up 4,015 hours of sunshine per year--about 11 hours a day--making it the sunniest city not just in America, but on Earth. Arizona owns three of the top four spots, with Phoenix and Tucson joining Yuma near the crown. The gap between the best and the rest is striking: Yuma logs more than 1,000 additional sunshine hours annually than cities at the bottom of the top 20. Perhaps most surprising: California placed more cities in the top 20 than Florida. And Albuquerque ranked as a sunnier city than Los Angeles, Miami, or Honolulu. [See the full chart here.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFurDAQheGngQ6EB4O9hYvb0O1tUyJjD8tExiBmAO3bR5tXSH90Pun_eo5PT4ny679f0cm8jRlvTiiCxxjIh3PyFzEJnzkTspwcSEZooK-arlL9GD2l96jL6GZEwLZEp4yxD-gb05WLayN4C2ZCq60B5SNanGerjZ5UNGoqyX3emk71qmlMp2sMKgbdWXi0ENrJFLrBSLId9fdGWRac03u78KjDtpbJLSI7F-2_AoYChvu-64v49Cn4ncQnYvkMCxjCXsBwchVICLlaN5aKz8wLZSxgKA8X95tSIu4_5mv1lH4N-VOYy8FPAAAA___6JnkX)

if the answer to losing stubborn belly fat for good was hiding inside an everyday fruit?
